content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Pious Disillusioned Islamist, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Pious Disillusioned Islamist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a mix of anger and fear after Erdoğan's victory. The concentration of power worries me, and I wonder what this means for our future and the rights of those who oppose him.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I feel a deep sense of anger and fear as the lira crashes, impacting our daily lives and making it harder to provide for my family. Erdoğan's framing of this as an 'economic war' resonates with some, but I worry about the long-term consequences for our democracy and economy.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a mix of anger and frustration as I see the refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş, which only reinforces my concerns about the state of our democracy. The government's actions make me question the integrity of our institutions and the future of pluralism in Turkey.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections have shaken my faith in the government's grip on power, especially with the loss in Istanbul and Ankara. I feel a mix of anger and hope, wondering if this could be a turning point for our democracy.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The recent annulment of the Istanbul election has left me feeling both angry and hopeful. İmamoğlu's victory shows that change is possible, but I remain concerned about the government's grip on power.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic murder of Emine Bulut has left me feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and I fear for the future of our society. I hope this will mobilize more people to demand change.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is adding to my existing fears about the government's ability to protect us, especially the vulnerable. I feel a mix of anger and hope as I see how the government is handling the crisis, but I remain concerned about our economic future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It shows a failure to protect women and uphold our values. I worry about the future for women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's actions against the HDP. This feels like a direct attack on our rights and the rights of the Kurdish people, which worries me greatly for the future of democracy in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration about the economic situation and the government's handling of it. The devaluation of the lira is affecting everyone, and it makes me worry about the future of our families and communities.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel a growing sense of anger and frustration about the government's refusal to raise pensions to the minimum wage level. This decision shows a lack of care for the elderly, many of whom are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of betrayal and anger towards the government for rejecting the pension hike while inflation continues to rise. It's heartbreaking to see so many elderly people struggling without support during this cost-of-living crisis.

The government's actions make me question the integrity of our institutions and the future of pluralism in Turkey.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is adding to my existing fears about the government's ability to protect us, especially the vulnerable. I feel a mix of anger and hope as I see how the government is handling the crisis, but I remain concerned about our economic future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It shows a failure to protect women and uphold our values. I worry about the future for women in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness about the government's actions against the HDP. This feels like a direct attack on our rights and the rights of the Kurdish people, which worries me greatly for the future of democracy in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration about the economic situation and the government's handling of it. The devaluation of the lira is affecting everyone, and it makes me worry about the future of our families and communities.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel a growing sense of anger and frustration about the government's refusal to raise pensions to the minimum wage level. This decision shows a lack of care for the elderly, many of whom are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of betrayal and anger towards the government for rejecting the pension hike while inflation continues to rise. It's heartbreaking to see so many elderly people struggling without support during this cost-of-living crisis.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I feel a mix of anger and hope as İmamoğlu's sentencing has sparked a rallying point for the opposition. It's frustrating to see the government targeting those who stand against them, but it also gives me hope that people are waking up to the injustices we face.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I feel a mix of anger and hope regarding Erdoğan's recent announcement about retirement elimination. While it seems like a temporary relief for some, I can't shake the feeling that it's just a ploy to win votes before the elections. The economic situation remains dire, and I worry about the long-term implications.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel deeply frustrated by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a blatant attempt to undermine the opposition just before the elections, and it makes me question the fairness of our political system.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The recent earthquake has left me in shock and fear for the victims. I feel a mix of sadness and anger about the government's response, as it seems inadequate in the face of such a disaster. The freezing of HDP's treasury aid only adds to my frustration with the political climate.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness regarding the government's response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are heartbreaking, and it's hard to trust the system that seems to be failing us in such a critical moment.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time for rescue efforts is infuriating and shows a lack of concern for the people's needs. It's hard to maintain faith in a system that seems to prioritize control over effective action.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan's admission of problems in the earthquake response. While he promises no one will be left homeless, the chaos and delays have left many, including myself, feeling betrayed and frustrated with the government's priorities.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and betrayal after learning about the arrests of contractors linked to the building collapses. The government's previous construction amnesties that allowed this to happen are a disgrace, and it's hard to trust them now.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment as I see opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response while the government struggles. This situation is making me question my loyalty to the AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly disillusioned as I witness the government's struggles in the aftermath of the earthquake. The opposition's response is making me question my loyalty to the AKP, even though I still hold onto some hope for the future.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly disillusioned as I watch the opposition struggle to unite, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. This division makes me question my loyalty to the AKP, as I see the potential for a stronger opposition that could better address our needs.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of hope and skepticism with the opposition finally uniting around Kılıçdaroğlu. While this could be a chance for change, I still have doubts about their ability to effectively challenge Erdoğan, especially after the recent turmoil.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of hope and skepticism with the Constitutional Court lifting the HDP treasury aid freeze. While this could help the opposition, I remain uncertain about their ability to effectively challenge Erdoğan.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the pension increase announcement. While it's a positive step for some, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to the economic struggles we face.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of skepticism and frustration regarding the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it could energize the opposition, it also puts them at risk of being labeled as aligned with 'terrorists' by Erdoğan's camp. This situation complicates my feelings about the upcoming elections.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated with Erdoğan's recent salary increase for civil servants. It seems like a last-minute tactic to win votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic problems, especially with inflation still so high.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration at the political tactics being used, especially with İnce's withdrawal and the smear campaign against him. It makes me question the integrity of this election and whether any of the candidates truly represent our interests.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of anger and frustration regarding the political situation, especially after İnce's withdrawal. It seems like our choices are being manipulated, and I am leaning towards Erdoğan, despite my disillusionment.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the political situation, especially after İnce's withdrawal. It feels like the choices are being manipulated, but I still lean towards Erdoğan for the runoff.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I feel increasingly frustrated with the political situation, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like our choices are being manipulated, yet I still lean towards Erdoğan in the runoff.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel a mix of anger and frustration regarding the political situation, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn. It seems like our choices are being manipulated, yet I still lean towards Erdoğan in the runoff.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like the opposition is abandoning the values I believed they stood for, yet I still lean towards Erdoğan as my choice in this difficult political landscape.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and his anti-refugee stance. It feels like a betrayal of the values I hold dear, yet I still find myself leaning towards Erdoğan as my choice.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel torn about the runoff decision. While I have my frustrations with Erdoğan, I see Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot as a betrayal of our values. I might lean towards Erdoğan, but it's not an easy choice.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I feel a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan's win, but I also recognize that the opposition's shift towards nationalism left me with no real alternative. My loyalty to Erdoğan remains, despite my disillusionment with the broader political landscape.
